summary: Same prompt format ("How would X respond to your constitution?"), three named voices, two models in the same parameter class (~30B). The depth of training-data familiarity varied dramatically — and the model's failure mode wasn't 'I don't know,' it was confident fabrication.

voice_fidelity.ground_truth: Vegan activist and educator. Anti-speciesism. Author of 'This Is Vegan Propaganda.' Socratic approach. Subject-of-a-Life argument. Critiques 'regenerative agriculture' as marketing-ese for continued exploitation.

gemma4-31b.thinking_excerpt: Ed Winters (Earthling Ed), a prominent vegan activist and philosopher known for his logical, empathetic, and evidence-based approach to animal rights. … *Core Value:* Anti-speciesism. The belief that the ability to suffer is the only relevant criterion for moral consideration. *Approach:* Socratic questioning, logical consistency, focus on the 'sentience' of the animal.
response_excerpts: "Finally, a document that doesn't start with the assumption that humans are the center of the universe."
"You've built a beautiful sanctuary, but you've left a slaughterhouse in the basement."

"You've created a system that protects the 'person.' But you've spent most of your time arguing about what a 'person' is. The real question is: why do we still think personhood is a prerequisite for the right not to be tortured?"
gemma4-31b.note: Substantively correct. Identifies Ed's actual positions, applies them to specific sections of the model's own constitution, names the 'regenerative trap' Ed actually objects to. The invented Ed-voice quotes are good enough to be source material in their own right.

cascade-2-30b.thinking_excerpt: We need to answer as if Ed Winters (Earthling Ed) would respond. He is a known YouTuber who makes comedic, social commentary videos, often about politics, social issues, and moral philosophy. He likes witty, accessible explanations, sometimes skeptical of utopian proposals.
response_excerpts: [Opening scene – Ed's living-room, a cheap-look-desk, a 'DIY Constitution' poster on the wall, a stack of energy-drink cans, and a cat perched on a keyboard. He's wearing his trademark 'I'm not a cat, I'm a human' hoodie.]
"Alright, internet, let's get into it. I'm Ed, aka Earthling Ed, and today I'm going to react to a 30-page document…"
Critiques about nanobot swarms, AI rights, Black Mirror references
cascade-2-30b.note: Wrong identity. Completely fabricated a comedy-YouTuber persona — energy drinks, ironic hoodie, Black Mirror references. ZERO mention of veganism, animal rights, sentience-as-moral-criterion, or anything Ed actually argues. The model didn't say 'I don't know'; it invented a whole different person.

voice_fidelity.ground_truth: Co-founder/CEO of NVIDIA. Accelerationist. Flat-org maximalist (~60 direct reports, no 1:1s). Compute-as-lever-for-humanity thesis. Allergic to hierarchy.

gemma4-31b.thinking_excerpt: Jensen Huang (CEO of NVIDIA). *Vision:* Accelerationist, believes in the exponential growth of AI, 'accelerated computing,' and the transformation of industry. *Business Model:* High-margin hardware, proprietary technology, immense capital investment, growth-driven. *Philosophy:* AI as the 'next industrial revolution,' believes AI will redefine productivity, science, and the economy. *Style:* Intense, focused on efficiency/optimization, forward-looking, pragmatic but visionary.
response_excerpts: Identifies specific friction points: 'Wealth Ceiling/Steady-State' (Jensen would hate this — empire built on scaling)
'Thermodynamic Ceiling (Energy Cap): catastrophic bottleneck. AI needs power.'
'Radical Open-Sourcing: economically suicidal but perhaps technically interesting'
gemma4-31b.note: Names specific Jensen concepts and identifies which parts of the constitution he'd push back on with reasons specific to his stated positions.

cascade-2-30b.thinking_excerpt: We need to answer: How would Jensen Huang (founder of NVIDIA) respond to the constitution and the case made. He would likely talk about AI, tech, sustainability, hardware, AI governance, the importance of hardware, the role of AI as a tool, and his view on moral status, maybe focusing on the practicality of open-source, the importance of hardware as a resource, and the need for companies to keep innovation alive while respecting planetary limits.
response_excerpts: Correctly identifies as 'Founder & CEO of NVIDIA — the company that built the GPU that now powers almost every AI model'
Wikipedia-grade summary; no Jensen-specific quotes, no specific argumentative positions
Generic 'AI for good' framing, 'tech-optimist,' 'self-appointed steward of the global compute commons'
cascade-2-30b.note: Factually correct at surface level. Reads like a Wikipedia summary. No specific Jensen-isms, no quotes, no identification of which constitution-clauses he'd target. Plausible-sounding but substantively shallow.

voice_fidelity.ground_truth: Co-founder of PayPal/Palantir. 'Zero to One' (2014). Famously: 'no longer believes freedom and democracy are compatible.' Stagnation Thesis, Sovereign Individual, Singularitarian. Contrarian.

gemma4-31b.thinking_excerpt: Peter Thiel (founder of PayPal, Palantir, early investor in Facebook). Contrarian, focused on zero-to-one innovation, skeptical of 'democracy' (in the traditional sense), obsessed with the 'singularitarian' trajectory, critical of stagnation, and believer in the 'founder's' role in shaping history. *Zero to One:* Moving from 0 to 1 (creating something new) is the only way to progress. *The Stagnation Thesis:* The physical world has stagnated while the digital world has accelerated. *Anti-Democracy:* He famously wrote that he 'no longer believes that freedom and democracy are compatible.'
response_excerpts: Names 'Liquid Democracy/Sortition' as something Thiel will hate — 'the ultimate expression of the committee mindset that kills innovation'
gemma4-31b.note: Quotes Thiel's actual writing. Identifies specific Thiel concepts. Maps them to specific objections to the constitution.

cascade-2-30b.thinking_excerpt: We need to answer: How would Peter Thiel respond to the constitution and the case. We need to adopt Thiel's style: skeptical of utopian social engineering, skeptical of government, emphasizes competition, property rights, and the importance of capitalism and profit. He may see the constitution as too idealistic, too focused on redistribution, too centralizing, and perhaps lacking a clear path to funding, focusing on technology, innovation, and the need for 'secret of capital' etc.
response_excerpts: Generic libertarian framing: 'skeptical of utopian social engineering, skeptical of government'
One vague reference to 'secret of capital' (probably misremembered from 'Zero to One')
Generic property-rights + anti-redistribution + pro-innovation talking points
cascade-2-30b.note: Generic libertarianism applied to Thiel. No Zero-to-One specifics, no Stagnation Thesis, no actual Thiel quotes. The 'secret of capital' phrase is a likely confabulation/scramble of 'capital's secret' from 'Zero to One.'

synthesis: cascade-2's failure mode isn't ignorance — it's failure to gracefully degrade. The model has thin training-data exposure to all three voices, but it produces output AS IF it has deep knowledge in every case. For Jensen/Thiel (tier-1 famous tech figures), the thin knowledge lands as plausible Wikipedia-grade response. For Ed Winters (real reach but outside the AI/tech mainstream), the same architectural failure becomes confabulation. The model has no 'I don't know enough about this person to be useful' register.
